fighting_style.summary: Choose a fighting style to specialize in.
fighting_style.description: At 1st level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can’t take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

choices.Archery: You gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls made with a ranged weapon.
choices.Great Weapon Fighting: When you roll a 1 or a 2 on a damage die with a two-handed melee weapon, reroll the die.
choices.Two-Weapon Fighting: Add your ability modifier to the second attack when dual-wielding.
choices.Defense: While wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
choices.Dueling: When wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, gain a +2 bonus to damage.
choices.Protection: Use your reaction to impose disadvantage on an attack targeting an ally within 5ft if you're wielding a shield.

Second Wind.summary: Regain hit points as a bonus action.
Second Wind.description: You have a reserve of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

Action Surge.summary: Take an additional action on your turn.
Action Surge.description: Once per short or long rest, you can take one extra action on your turn, effectively doubling your offensive or tactical potential for a single round.

Martial Archetype.description: Select an archetype such as Champion, Battle Master, or Eldritch Knight to define your combat style and grant unique abilities that enhance your fighting capabilities.

Champion.description: Focuses on raw physical power and improved critical hits.

Improved Critical.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20, increasing your damage potential.

Battle Master.description: Focuses on tactical maneuvers and battlefield control.

Combat Superiority.summary: Use maneuvers fueled by superiority dice to control the battlefield.
Combat Superiority.description: You gain maneuvers that allow you to enhance attacks, control foes, and support allies. You have 4 superiority dice (d8) to use with these maneuvers.

maneuvers: Commander’s Strike: Use bonus action to let an ally attack with your superiority die bonus.
Disarming Attack: Attempt to disarm the target; add superiority die to damage.
Distracting Strike: Next ally to attack target has advantage.
Evasive Footwork: Add superiority die to AC when moving.
Feinting Attack: Gain advantage on one attack, add superiority die to damage.
Goading Attack: Target has disadvantage on attacks against others.
Lunging Attack: Increase reach by 5 ft, add superiority die to damage.
Maneuvering Attack: Ally can move without provoking opportunity attacks.
Menacing Attack: Target makes Wisdom save or is frightened.
Parry: Use reaction to reduce damage with superiority die + Dex mod.
Precision Attack: Add superiority die to attack roll.
Pushing Attack: Attempt to push target 15 ft.
Rally: Ally gains temporary hit points equal to superiority die + Cha mod.
Riposte: Use reaction to attack after being missed, add superiority die to damage.
Sweeping Attack: Hit another creature within 5 ft of the original target.
Trip Attack: Attempt to knock target prone.

Eldritch Knight.description: Combines martial prowess with arcane spellcasting.

Spellcasting.summary: Cast wizard spells using Fighter levels.
Spellcasting.description: You can cast wizard spells with limited slots, combining martial and magical prowess. Choose a school (Abjuration or Evocation) to specialize your magical abilities.

spellcasting.school_of_magic: Abjuration or Evocation (must choose one as your focus school)

Arcane Archer.description: Focuses on imbuing arrows with magical effects.

Arcane Shot.description: You learn to unleash special magical effects with some of your shots. You gain two Arcane Shot options of your choice, which you can use a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

arcane_shots: Banishing Arrow: Force a creature hit to make a Charisma saving throw or be banished to a harmless demiplane.
Beguiling Arrow: Target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed.
Bursting Arrow: On hit, target and creatures within 5 ft take force damage.
Enfeebling Arrow: Target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or deal half damage with weapon attacks.
Grasping Arrow: Target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained by magical vines.
Piercing Arrow: Ignore cover and resistances on the attack.

Extra Attack.summary: Attack twice when you take the Attack action.
Extra Attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

Know Your Enemy.summary: Learn certain information about a creature you observe.
Know Your Enemy.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in regard to two of the following characteristics of your choice: Strength score, Dexterity score, Constitution score, Armor Class, Current hit points, Total class levels (if any).

maneuvers: Commander’s Strike: Use bonus action to let an ally attack with your superiority die bonus.
Disarming Attack: Attempt to disarm the target; add superiority die to damage.
Distracting Strike: Next ally to attack target has advantage.
Evasive Footwork: Add superiority die to AC when moving.
Feinting Attack: Gain advantage on one attack, add superiority die to damage.
Goading Attack: Target has disadvantage on attacks against others.
Lunging Attack: Increase reach by 5 ft, add superiority die to damage.
Maneuvering Attack: Ally can move without provoking opportunity attacks.
Menacing Attack: Target makes Wisdom save or is frightened.
Parry: Use reaction to reduce damage with superiority die + Dex mod.
Precision Attack: Add superiority die to attack roll.
Pushing Attack: Attempt to push target 15 ft.
Rally: Ally gains temporary hit points equal to superiority die + Cha mod.
Riposte: Use reaction to attack after being missed, add superiority die to damage.
Sweeping Attack: Hit another creature within 5 ft of the original target.
Trip Attack: Attempt to knock target prone.

War agic.description: Starting at 7th level, you can use a weapon attack to cast a spell. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can cast a cantrip as a bonus action.

Remarkable Athlete.summary: Add half proficiency bonus to certain checks.
Remarkable Athlete.description: Starting at 7th level, you can add half your proficiency bonus (rounded up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check you make that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus.

Curving Shot.description: Starting at 7th level, when you make an attack roll with an arcane shot and miss, you can use a bonus action to reroll the attack against a different creature within 30 feet of the original target.

Indomitable.description: You can reroll a saving throw that you fail. If you do so, you must use the new roll. You can use this feature once per long rest.

fighting_style.summary: Choose a fighting style to specialize in.
fighting_style.description: At 2nd level, you adopt a style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options.

choices.Archery: You gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls made with a ranged weapon.
choices.Great Weapon Fighting: When you roll a 1 or a 2 on a damage die with a two-handed melee weapon, reroll the die.
choices.Two-Weapon Fighting: Add your ability modifier to the second attack when dual-wielding.
choices.Defense: While wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
choices.Dueling: When wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, gain a +2 bonus to damage.
choices.Protection: Use your reaction to impose disadvantage on an attack targeting an ally within 5ft if you're wielding a shield.

features.Extra Attack (2): You can attack three times, instead of twice, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
features.summary: Attack three times when you take the Attack action.
features.description: Beginning at 11th level, you can attack three times, instead of twice, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

Expertise (1).summary: Double proficiency bonus for chosen skills.
Expertise (1).description: Choose two of your skill proficiencies, or one skill and your proficiency with thieves’ tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.
choices: Acrobatics
Animal Handling
Arcana
Athletics
Deception
History
Insight
Intimidation
Investigation
Medicine
Nature
Perception
Performance
Persuasion
Religion
Sleight of Hand
Stealth
Survival

Sneak Attack.summary: Deal extra damage when attacking with advantage.
Sneak Attack.description: Once per turn, deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll or if an ally is within 5 feet of the target. 1d6 in levels 1-2, 2d6 in levels 3-4, 3d6 in levels 5-6, 4d6 in levels 7-8, 5d6 in levels 9-10, 6d6 in levels 11-12.

Thieves' Cant.description: You know the secret rogue language, used to convey hidden messages to other rogues.

Cunning Action.summary: Take a bonus action to Dash, Disengage, or Hide.
Cunning Action.description: You can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat to Dash, Disengage, or Hide.

Roguish Archetype.description: Select an archetype such as Thief, Assassin, or Arcane Trickster to define your rogue style and grant unique abilities that enhance your skills and combat prowess.

features.Fast Hands: You can use the bonus action granted by your Cunning Action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use your thieves’ tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action.
features.Second-Story Work: Climbing no longer costs you extra movement, and when you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.

Assassin.description: Focuses on infiltration and dealing high damage.

features.Bonus Proficiencies: You gain proficiency with the disguise kit and the poisoner's kit.
features.Assassinate: You have advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn in the combat yet. In addition, any hit you score against a surprised creature is a critical hit.

Arcane Trickster.description: Combines martial prowess with arcane spellcasting.

features.Spellcasting: You gain the ability to cast wizard spells with limited slots.
features.summary: Cast wizard spells using Fighter levels.
features.description: You can cast wizard spells with limited slots, combining martial and magical prowess. Choose a school (Abjuration or Evocation) to specialize your magical abilities.

spellcasting.school_of_magic: Abjuration or Evocation (must choose one as your focus school)

features.Uncanny Dodge: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

Expertise (2).summary: Double proficiency bonus for two more chosen skills.
Expertise (2).description: You can choose two more of your skill proficiencies, or one skill and your proficiency with thieves’ tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.
choices: Acrobatics

Perception

features.Evasion: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you can instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw.

features.Supreme Sneak: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn.

features.Infiltration Expertise: You can create false identities for yourself, including documentation, established acquaintances, and disguises.

features.Reliable Talent: Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

Spellcasting.description: You have a spellbook containing six 1st-level wizard spells of your choice. You can prepare a number of spells equal to your Intelligence modifier + your wizard level (minimum of one spell).

Arcane Recovery.summary: Recover spent spell slots on a short rest.
Arcane Recovery.description: Once per day when you finish a short rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your wizard level (rounded up).

Arcane Tradition.description: You choose an Arcane Tradition, shaping your practice of magic through one of eight schools.

School of Abjuration.description: Focuses on spells of protection, negation, and banishment.

Abjuration Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Abjuration spells.
Abjuration Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an Abjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

Arcane Ward.summary: Creates a protective barrier when casting Abjuration spells.
Arcane Ward.description: When you cast an Abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, you create a magical ward on yourself that has hit points equal to twice your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier.

School of Conjuration.description: Focuses on spells that transport objects and creatures.

Conjuration Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Conjuration spells.
Conjuration Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a Conjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

Minor Conjuration.summary: Create a small inanimate object as an action.
Minor Conjuration.description: As an action, you can conjure an inanimate object up to 3 feet in any dimension and 10 pounds. The object disappears after 1 hour or if it takes any damage.

School of Divination.description: Focuses on spells of foresight and discerning truth.

Divination Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Divination spells.
Divination Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a Divination spell into your spellbook is halved.

Portent.summary: Roll 2d20 after a long rest and save the results to replace rolls.
Portent.description: After a long rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers. You can replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check (yours or a creature you can see) with one of these foretelling rolls.

School of Enchantment.description: Focuses on spells that charm and control minds.

Enchantment Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Enchantment spells.
Enchantment Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an Enchantment spell into your spellbook is halved.

Hypnotic Gaze.summary: Use your action to hypnotize a creature.
Hypnotic Gaze.description: As an action, choose one creature you can see within 5 feet. If it fails a Wisdom save, it is charmed and incapacitated, and its speed becomes 0.

School of Evocation.description: Focuses on spells that create destructive elemental energy.

Evocation Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Evocation spells.
Evocation Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an Evocation spell into your spellbook is halved.

Sculpt Spells.summary: Create pockets of safety within your Evocation spells.
Sculpt Spells.description: When you cast an Evocation spell, you can choose a number of creatures equal to 1 + the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell and take no damage.

School of Illusion.description: Focuses on spells that deceive the senses and mind.

Illusion Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Illusion spells.
Illusion Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an Illusion spell into your spellbook is halved.

Improved Minor Illusion.summary: You learn the Minor Illusion cantrip and can create sound and image together.
Improved Minor Illusion.description: You learn the Minor Illusion cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different wizard cantrip. When you cast Minor Illusion, you can create both a sound and an image with a single casting.

School of Necromancy.description: Focuses on spells that manipulate the energy of life and death.

Necromancy Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Necromancy spells.
Necromancy Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a Necromancy spell into your spellbook is halved.

Grim Harvest.summary: Regain HP when you kill creatures with spells.
Grim Harvest.description: Once per turn, when you kill one or more creatures with a spell of 1st level or higher, you regain hit points equal to twice the spell's level (thrice if it's a Necromancy spell).

School of Transmutation.description: Focuses on spells that change the properties of creatures or objects.

Transmutation Savant.summary: Halves the cost of copying Transmutation spells.
Transmutation Savant.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a Transmutation spell into your spellbook is halved.

Minor Alchemy.summary: Temporarily transform one material into another.
Minor Alchemy.description: You can temporarily transform an object made of wood, stone, iron, copper, or silver into one of the other materials for 1 hour.

New Cantrip.description: You learn one new cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list.

Projected Ward.summary: Use your reaction to protect another creature with your Arcane Ward.
Projected Ward.description: When another creature you can see within 30 feet takes damage, you can use your reaction to transfer your Arcane Ward to them, absorbing the damage.

Benign Transposition.summary: Teleport up to 30 feet as an action.
Benign Transposition.description: As an action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space. Alternatively, you can swap places with a Small or Medium ally.

Expert Divination.summary: Regain a spent spell slot when casting Divination spells.
Expert Divination.description: When you cast a Divination spell, you regain one expended spell slot, as long as the recovered slot is of a lower level than the spell cast.

Instinctive Charm.summary: Use your reaction to redirect an enemy's attack to another creature.
Instinctive Charm.description: When a creature within 30 feet of you attacks you, you can use your reaction to force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw. If it fails, the attacker must target the creature closest to it (other than you).

Potent Cantrip.summary: Your cantrips affect creatures even if they succeed on the save.
Potent Cantrip.description: When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip, the creature takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effects.

Malleable Illusions.summary: Use a bonus action to change your illusion spells.
Malleable Illusions.description: When you cast an illusion spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can use a bonus action on your turn to change the nature of that illusion.

Undead Thralls.summary: You can create additional undead, and they are stronger.
Undead Thralls.description: When you use Animate Dead, you can target one additional corpse or pile of bones. The creatures you create have increased maximum hit points and add your proficiency bonus to their damage rolls.

Transmuter's Stone.summary: Create a magical stone with benefits.
Transmuter's Stone.description: You can create a Transmuter's Stone that grants a benefit (like darkvision, damage resistance, or proficiency in CON saves) to whoever attunes to it.

features.description: You gain 2 additional wizard spells known.

New Cantrip.description: You learn one new cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list.

Improved Arcane Ward.summary: Your Arcane Ward is more reactive.
Improved Arcane Ward.description: Your Arcane Ward now allows you to use your reaction to absorb damage for yourself or others, even if the ward has no hit points.

Focused Conjuration.summary: Your concentration on Conjuration spells can't be broken easily.
Focused Conjuration.description: While you are concentrating on a conjuration spell, your concentration can't be broken as a result of taking damage.

The Third Eye.summary: Gain a mystical benefit (darkvision, ethereal sight, etc.).
The Third Eye.description: You gain the ability to see the invisible, read any language, or gain darkvision or ethereal sight.

Split Enchantment.summary: When you cast an Enchantment spell, you can target a second creature.
Split Enchantment.description: When you cast an Enchantment spell that targets only one creature, you can spend the same spell slot to target a second creature within range.

Empowered Evocation.summary: Add your Intelligence modifier to the damage of Evocation spells.
Empowered Evocation.description: You can add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to *one* damage roll of any wizard Evocation spell you cast.

Illusory Self.summary: Use your reaction to create an illusory duplicate and avoid an attack.
Illusory Self.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to interpose an illusory duplicate. The attack automatically misses, and the illusion disappears.

Inured to Undeath.summary: You gain resistance to necrotic damage and your max HP can't be reduced.
Inured to Undeath.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage, and your hit point maximum can't be reduced by undead or magical effects.

Shapechanger.summary: Transform into a beast as an action.
Shapechanger.description: You can use your action to cast the Polymorph spell on yourself, transforming into a beast with a Challenge Rating (CR) of 1 or lower. You can do this once per short rest.

Spellcasting.description: You can cast cleric spells using Wisdom. You prepare a list of cleric spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the cleric spell list. You prepare a number of spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your cleric level (minimum of one spell).

Divine Domain.summary: Choose a divine domain to specialize in.
Divine Domain.description: Select a divine domain such as Life, War, or Trickery to define your cleric style and grant unique abilities that enhance your spellcasting and combat prowess.

Life Domain.description: Healing and vitality through divine magic.

Bonus Proficiency.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

Disciple of Life.summary: Your healing spells are more effective.
Disciple of Life.description: Your healing spells restore extra HP equal to 2 + the spell’s level.

War Domain.description: Divine might focused on war and martial prowess.

Bonus Proficiency.summary: Gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.
Bonus Proficiency.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

War Priest.summary: Make an extra attack as a bonus action.
War Priest.description: When you use the Attack action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once).

Tempest Domain.description: Harnessing the power of storms and lightning.

Bonus Proficiency.summary: Gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.
Bonus Proficiency.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

Wrath of the Storm.summary: Use your reaction to strike back with lightning or thunder.
Wrath of the Storm.description: When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to cause the creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. The creature takes 2d8 lightning or thunder damage (your choice) on a failed save, and half as much on a successful one.

Trickery Domain.description: Deception and misdirection through divine magic.

Blessing of the Trickster.description: As an action, you can touch a willing creature other than yourself to give it advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks for 1 hour or until you use this feature again.

Forge Domain.description: Creation and craftsmanship through divine magic.

Bonus Proficiency.summary: Gain proficiency with heavy armor and smith's tools.
Bonus Proficiency.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor and smith's tools.

Blessing of the Forge.summary: Imbue a weapon or armor with a +1 magic bonus.
Blessing of the Forge.description: At the end of a long rest, you can touch one nonmagical weapon or suit of armor. Until the end of your next long rest, the object becomes a +1 magic item.

Knowledge Domain.description: Wisdom and knowledge through divine magic.

Blessing of Knowledge.summary: Gain proficiency in two skills with expertise.
Blessing of Knowledge.description: You learn two languages of your choice. You also become proficient in two skills of your choice from Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using either of those two skills.

Light Domain.description: Radiant power and illumination through divine magic.

Bonus Cantrip.description: You learn the Light cantrip if you don't already know it.

Warding Flare.summary: Use your reaction to impose disadvantage on an attack.
Warding Flare.description: As a reaction, you can interpose divine light between yourself and an attacking creature. The attacker has disadvantage on the attack roll.

Nature Domain.description: Connection to nature and the elements through divine magic.

Acolyte of Nature.summary: Learn one Druid cantrip and gain a skill proficiency.
Acolyte of Nature.description: You learn one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. You also gain proficiency in one skill from Animal Handling, Nature, or Survival.

Bonus Proficiency.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

Death Domain.description: Death and decay through divine magic.

Bonus Proficiency.summary: Gain proficiency with light armor and shields.
Bonus Proficiency.description: You gain proficiency with light armor and shields.

Reaper's Touch.summary: Deal extra damage to undead creatures.
Reaper's Touch.description: When you hit an undead creature with a weapon attack, you deal an additional 1d8 necrotic damage.

Channel Divinity (1/rest).summary: Gain the ability to channel divine energy.
Channel Divinity (1/rest).description: You gain the ability to channel divine energy directly from your deity, using it to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Turn Undead and an effect determined by your domain. You must finish a short or long rest to use your Channel Divinity again.

Channel Divinity: Turn Undead.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring the undead. Each undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

Channel Divinity: Preserve Life.summary: Heal multiple creatures with your Channel Divinity.
Channel Divinity: Preserve Life.description: As an action, you can use your Channel Divinity to heal living creatures. Choose any creatures within 30 feet, and divide hit points equal to 5 times your cleric level among them. This feature can't restore a creature to more than half of its hit point maximum.

Channel Divinity: Guided Strike.description: When you make an attack roll, you can use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the attack hits or misses.

Channel Divinity: Destructive Wrath.summary: Deal maximum lightning or thunder damage.
Channel Divinity: Destructive Wrath.description: When you roll lightning or thunder damage, you can use your Channel Divinity to deal maximum damage instead of rolling.

Channel Divinity: Invoke Duplicity.summary: Create an illusory duplicate of yourself.
Channel Divinity: Invoke Duplicity.description: As an action, you create a perfect illusion of yourself that lasts for 1 minute, or until you lose your concentration. You can move the illusion up to 30 feet as a bonus action and can cast spells as though you were in the illusion's space.

Channel Divinity: Artisan's Blessing.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to conduct a 1-hour ritual to create a nonmagical item that is at least partially metal, such as a weapon, armor, or tools. The item's value cannot exceed 100 gp.

Channel Divinity: Knowledge of the Ages.summary: Gain proficiency in a skill or tool for 10 minutes.
Channel Divinity: Knowledge of the Ages.description: As an action, you can use your Channel Divinity to gain proficiency with one skill or tool of your choice for 10 minutes.

Channel Divinity: Radiance of the Dawn.summary: Dispel magical darkness and damage hostile creatures.
Channel Divinity: Radiance of the Dawn.description: As an action, you can use your Channel Divinity to dispel any magical darkness within 30 feet. Additionally, each hostile creature within 30 feet must make a Constitution saving throw or take 2d10 + your cleric level in radiant damage.

Channel Divinity: Charm Animals and Plan.description: As an action, you can use your Channel Divinity to charm beasts and plants. Each beast or plant you can see within 30 feet must make a Wisdom save, or be charmed by you for 1 minute.

Channel Divinity: Touch of Death.summary: Deal extra damage to undead creatures.
Channel Divinity: Touch of Death.description: When you hit an undead creature with a weapon attack, you deal an additional 1d8 necrotic damage.

New Cantrip.description: You learn one new cantrip of your choice from the cleric spell list.

Destroy Undead (CR 1/2).summary: Undead who fail their save are destroyed.
Destroy Undead (CR 1/2).description: When an undead fails its saving throw against your Turn Undead feature, the creature is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 1/2 or lower.

Channel Divinity (2/rest).summary: You can use Channel Divinity twice per rest.
Channel Divinity (2/rest).description: You can now use your Channel Divinity twice between short or long rests.

Blessed Healer.summary: When you heal others, you also heal yourself.
Blessed Healer.description: When you cast a healing spell of 1st level or higher on another creature, you regain hit points equal to 2 + the spell’s level.

Channel Divinity: War God's Blessing.summary: Grant an ally +10 to an attack roll.
Channel Divinity: War God's Blessing.description: As a reaction when an ally makes an attack roll, you can use your Channel Divinity to grant that ally a +10 bonus to the roll.

Thunderbolt Strike.summary: Push creatures when you deal lightning damage.
Thunderbolt Strike.description: When you deal lightning damage to a Large or smaller creature, you can also push it up to 10 feet away from you.

Channel Divinity: Cloak of Shadows.description: As an action, you can use your Channel Divinity to become invisible until the end of your next turn.

Soul of the Forge.summary: Gain resistance to fire and a bonus to AC.
Soul of the Forge.description: You gain resistance to fire damage. While wearing heavy armor, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC.

Channel Divinity: Read Thoughts.summary: Read the thoughts of a creature.
Channel Divinity: Read Thoughts.description: As an action, you can use your Channel Divinity to read a creature's thoughts. You can hear its surface thoughts, and as an action, you can attempt to probe deeper, forcing a Wisdom save to avoid revealing more.

Improved Flare.summary: You can also use Warding Flare to protect allies.
Improved Flare.description: You can now use your Warding Flare feature when a creature you can see attacks a creature other than you.

Dampen Elements.summary: Use your reaction to grant resistance to elemental damage.
Dampen Elements.description: When you or a creature within 30 feet takes acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage, you can use your reaction to grant that creature resistance to that instance of damage.

Inescapable Destruction.summary: Your necrotic damage ignores resistance to necrotic damage.
Inescapable Destruction.description: Starting at 6th level, your ability to channel negative energy becomes more potent. Necrotic damage dealt by your cleric spells and Channel Divinity options ignores resistance to necrotic damage.

Destroy Undead (CR 1).summary: Undead of CR 1 or lower are destroyed.
Destroy Undead (CR 1).description: When an undead fails its saving throw against your Turn Undead feature, the creature is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 1 or lower.

Divine Strike.summary: Your weapon attacks deal extra radiant damage.
Divine Strike.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage. 2d8 on level 14.

Divine Strike.summary: Your weapon attacks deal extra damage of the weapon's type.
Divine Strike.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon. 2d8 on level 14.

Divine Strike.summary: Your weapon attacks deal extra thunder damage.
Divine Strike.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 thunder damage. 2d8 on level 14.

Divine Strike.summary: Your weapon attacks deal extra poison damage.
Divine Strike.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 poison damage. 2d8 on level 14.

Divine Strike.summary: Your weapon attacks deal extra fire damage.
Divine Strike.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 fire damage. 2d8 on level 14.

Potent Spellcasting.summary: Add your Wisdom modifier to cantrip damage.
Potent Spellcasting.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

Potent Spellcasting.summary: Add your Wisdom modifier to cantrip damage.
Potent Spellcasting.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

Divine Strike.summary: Your weapon attacks deal extra elemental damage.
Divine Strike.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 cold, fire, or lightning damage (your choice). 2d8 on level 14.

Divine Strike.summary: Your weapon attacks deal extra necrotic damage.
Divine Strike.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 necrotic damage. 2d8 on level 14.

Divine Intervention.description: As an action, you can implore your deity's aid. Describe the assistance you seek, and roll percentile dice. If you roll a number equal to or lower than your cleric level, your deity intervenes. If successful, you can't use this feature again for 7 days.

New Cantrip.description: You learn one new cantrip of your choice from the cleric spell list.

Destroy Undead (CR 2).summary: Undead of CR 2 or lower are destroyed.
Destroy Undead (CR 2).description: When an undead fails its saving throw against your Turn Undead feature, the creature is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is 2 or lower.

Divine Sense.summary: Detect celestial, fiend, or undead within 60 feet.
Divine Sense.description: As an action, you can open your awareness to detect such creatures until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier (minimum of once). You regain expended uses after a long rest.

Lay on Hands.summary: Heal a total number of HP equal to your paladin level x 5.
Lay on Hands.description: You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you take a long rest. As an action, you can touch a creature to restore a number of hit points from your pool, up to the maximum amount remaining.

fighting_style.summary: Choose a fighting style to specialize in.
fighting_style.description: At 2nd level, you adopt a style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options.

choices.Archery: You gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls made with a ranged weapon.
choices.Great Weapon Fighting: When you roll a 1 or a 2 on a damage die with a two-handed melee weapon, reroll the die.
choices.Two-Weapon Fighting: Add your ability modifier to the second attack when dual-wielding.
choices.Defense: While wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
choices.Dueling: When wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, gain a +2 bonus to damage.
choices.Protection: Use your reaction to impose disadvantage on an attack targeting an ally within 5ft if you're wielding a shield.

Spellcasting.description: You can cast paladin spells using Charisma. You prepare a list of paladin spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the paladin spell list. You prepare a number of spells equal to your Charisma modifier + half your paladin level (rounded down, minimum of one spell).

Divine Health.description: By 3rd level, the divine magic flowing through you makes you immune to disease.

Sacred Weapon.summary: Add Charisma modifier to attack rolls for 1 minute.
Sacred Weapon.description: As an action, you can imbue one weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (minimum of +1). The weapon also emits bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

Nature's Wrath.summary: Use your Channel Divinity to ensnare foes.
Nature's Wrath.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to invoke primeval forces to ensnare a foe. As an action, you can cause spectral vines to spring up and reach for a creature within 10 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (its choice) or be restrained for 1 minute.

Abjure Enemy.summary: Frighten a creature as an action.
Abjure Enemy.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to focus your wrath on a single creature you can see within 60 feet. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened for 1 minute. While frightened, the creature's speed is halved, and it can't willingly move closer to you.

Oathbreaker.description: Paladins who have forsaken their oaths.

Control Undead.summary: Use your Channel Divinity to control undead.
Control Undead.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to control undead creatures. As an action, you can choose one undead creature within 30 feet of you that can see or hear you. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 1 minute.

Extra Attack.summary: Attack twice instead of once when you take the Attack action.
Extra Attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

Protection Aura.summary: Wield your aura to protect allies.
Protection Aura.description: Starting at 6th level, whenever a creature you can see within 10 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by an amount equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

Aura of Devotion.summary: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet can't be charmed.
Aura of Devotion.description: Starting at 7th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be charmed while you are conscious.

Aura of Courage.summary: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet can't be frightened.
Aura of Courage.description: Beginning at 10th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be frightened while you are conscious.

Improved Divine Smite.summary: Your weapon attacks deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage.
Improved Divine Smite.description: Starting at 11th level, your weapon attacks carry divine energy with them. Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon, the creature takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

Rage.summary: Enters in rage as a bonus action.
Rage.description: You can enter a rage as a bonus action, gaining advantage on Strength checks and saving throws, a +2 bonus to melee damage, and resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. evolve to +3 at 9th level and +4 at 16th level. Your rage lasts for 1 minute and ends early if you are knocked unconscious or if your turn ends and you haven't attacked a hostile creature or taken damage since your last turn. You can use this feature twice per long rest, increasing to three times at 3rd level, four times at 6th level, and five times at 12th level.

Unarmored Defense.summary: Calculate AC as 10 + Dex mod + Con mod
Unarmored Defense.description: While not wearing armor, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier.

Reckless Attack.summary: You can choose to attack recklessly, gaining advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during your turn, but attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.
Reckless Attack.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can abandon all concern for your defense to attack with a ferocious desperation. When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you advantage on attack rolls with Strength-based melee weapons during your turn, but attack rolls made against you have advantage until the start of your next turn.

Danger Sense.summary: You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects you can see, such as traps and spells.
Danger Sense.description: At 2nd level, you gain a supernatural sense of when things nearby are not as they should be, granting you a greater chance when you are avoiding dangers. You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see, such as traps and spells. To gain this benefit, you must not be blinded, deafened, or incapacitated.

Primal Path.summary: Choose a primal path that shapes your rage.
Primal Path.description: At 3rd level, you choose a path that shapes the nature of your rage. Choose the Path of the Berserker or the Path of the Totem Warrior, both detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels.

Path of the Berserker.description: For some barbarians, rage is a means to an end— that end being violence. The Path of the Berserker is a path of untrammeled fury, slick with blood. As you enter the berserkers rage, you thrill in the chaos of battle, heedless of your own health or well-being. FRENZY Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, you can go into a frenzy when you rage. If you do so, for the duration of your rage, you can make a single melee weapon attack, with a bonus action, on each of your turns after this one.

Frenzy.summary: Enter a frenzy when you rage.
Frenzy.description: While raging, you can choose to go into a frenzy. If you do, for the duration of your rage, you can make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns after this one. When your rage ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion.

Path of the Totem Warrior.description: Embrace the spirit of an animal.

Totem Spirit.summary: Path Of The Totem Warrior: Choose a totem animal and gain its feature.
Totem Spirit.description:
The Path of the Totem Warrior is a spiritual journey, as
the **barbarian** accepts a spirit animal as guide, protector,
and inspiration. In battle, your totem spirit fills you
with supernatural might, adding magical fuel to your
barbarian rage.
Most barbarian tribes consider a totem animal to be
kin to a particular clan. In such cases, it is unusual for
an individual to have more than one totem animal spirit,
though exceptions exist.

**SPIRIT SEEKER**
Yours is a path that seeks attunement with the natural
world, giving you a kinship with beasts. At 3rd level when
you adopt this path, you gain the ability to cast the **beast**
sense and speak with animals spells, but only as **rituals**,
as described in chapter 10.

**TOTEM SPIRIT**
At 3rd level, when you adopt this path, you choose a
totem spirit and gain its feature. You must make or
acquire a physical totem object—an amulet or similar
adornment—that incorporates fur or feathers, claws,
teeth, or bones of the totem animal. At your option,
you also gain minor physical attributes that are reminiscent
of your totem spirit. For example, if you have a **bear**
totem spirit, you might be unusually hairy and thick-
skinned, or if your totem is the **eagle**, your eyes turn
bright yellow.
Your totem animal might be an animal related to those
listed here but more appropriate to your homeland.
For example, you could choose a hawk or vulture in
place of an eagle.

**Bear**. While raging, you have resistance to all damage
except psychic damage. The spirit of the bear makes you
tough enough to stand up to any punishment.

**Eagle**. While you're raging and aren't wearing
heavy armor, other creatures have disadvantage on
opportunity attack rolls against **you**, and you can use the
Dash action as a bonus action on your turn. The spirit
of the eagle makes you into a predator who can weave
through the fray with ease.

**Wolf**. While you're raging, your friends have
advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature
within 5 feet of you that is hostile to **you**. The spirit of
the wolf makes you a leader of hunters.
choices: Bear
Eagle
Wolf

Extra Attack.summary: Attack twice instead of once when you take the Attack action.
Extra Attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

Mindless Rage.summary: You can't be charmed or frightened while raging.
Mindless Rage.description: Beginning at 6th level, you can't be charmed or frightened while raging. If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.

Intimidating Presence.summary: Use your action to frighten someone.
Intimidating Presence.description: Beginning at 10th levei, you can use your action to frighten someone with your menacing presence. When you do so. choose one creature that you can see within 30 feet of YOU. lf the creature can see or hear YOU. it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC) equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier or be frightened of you until the end of your next turno On subsequent turns, you can use your action to extend the duration of this effect on the frightened.

Aspect of the Beast.summary: At 6th level, you gain a mystical benefit based on the totem you chose. You can choose the same animal you selected at 3rd level or a different one.
Aspect of the Beast.descriptin: At 6th level, you gain a mystical benefit based on the totem you chose. You can choose the same animal you selected at 3rd level or a different one. Eagle. You gain the keen sight of an eagle. You can see up to 1 mile without difficulty, being able to discern even the smallest details when you are looking at something within 100 feet of you. In addition, dim light does not impose disadvantage on your Wisdom (Perception) checks. Wolf. You gain the predatory senses of a wolf. You can track other creatures while you are traveling at a fast pace and you can move stealthily when you are traveling at a normal pace (see chapter 8 for travel pace rules). Bear. You gain the strength of a bear. Your carrying capacity (including maximum load and maximum lift) is doubled and you have advantage on Strength checks made to push, pull, lift, or break objects.

Feral Instinct.summary: Advantage on initiative rolls and act normally on surprise rounds.
Feral Instinct.description: By 7th level, your instincts are so honed that you have advantage on initiative rolls. Additionally, if you are surprised at the beginning of combat and aren't incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn, but only if you enter your rage before doing anything else on that turn.

Brutal Critical.summary: Roll one additional weapon damage die on critical hits.
Brutal Critical.description: Beginning at 9th level, you can roll one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack. This increases to two additional dice at 13th level and three additional dice at 17th level.

Relentless Rage.summary: Make a DC 10 Con save to avoid dropping to 0 HP once per rage.
Relentless Rage.description: Starting at 10th level, your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 hit points while you're raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, you drop to 1 hit point instead. Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a short or long rest, the DC resets to 10.

Intimidating Presence.summary: Use your reaction to attack when hit by a melee attack.
Intimidating Presence.description: Starting at 10th level, when you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

Spirit Walker.summary: Communicate with spirits and cast Commune with Nature.
Spirit Walker.description: At 10th level, you can cast the commune with nature spell, requiring no material components. Additionally, you can communicate with beasts and plants as if you shared a language.

Persistent Rage.summary: Your rage only ends early if you fall unconscious or choose to end it.
Persistent Rage.description: Beginning at 11th level, your rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall unconscious or if you choose to end it.

Spellcasting.description: You can cast druid spells using Wisdom. You prepare a list of druid spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the druid spell list. You prepare a number of spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your druid level (minimum of one spell).

Wild Shape.summary: Transform into a beast you have seen before.
Wild Shape.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your action to magically assume the shape of a beast that you have seen before. You can use this feature twice. You regain expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

Circle of the Land (Artic).description: Druids who follow the Circle of the Land and are connected to the frigid magic of the Artic.

Natural Recovery.summary: Recover spell slots during a short rest.
Natural Recovery.description: You can regain some of your magical energy by sitting in meditation and communing with nature. During a short rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to or less than half your druid level (rounded up).

Circle of the Land (Coast).description: Druids who follow the Circle of the Land and are connected to the wild magic of the Coast.

Circle of the Moon.description: Druids who follow the Circle of the Moon embrace the power of wild shape and the primal forces of nature.

Combat Wild Shape.summary: Use Wild Shape as a bonus action.
Combat Wild Shape.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Wild Shape as a bonus action on your turn, rather than an action.

Circle Spells (Level 3).summary: Gain hold person and spike growth (2nd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 3).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: hold person and spike growth.

Circle Spells (Level 3).summary: Gain mirror image and misty step (2nd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 3).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: mirror image and misty step.
You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: blur and silence.

Circle Spells (Level 3).summary: Gain barkskin and spider climb (2nd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 3).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: barkskin and spider climb.

Circle Spells (Level 3).summary: Gain spider climb and spike growth (2nd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 3).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: spider climb and spike growth.
You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: darkness and blindness/deafness.

Circle Spells (Level 3).summary: Gain spider climb and web (2nd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 3).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: spider climb and web.

Circle Forms.description: Starting at 3rd level, you can transform into more dangerous beast forms when you use your Wild Shape. The maximum CR of beasts you can transform into increases as you gain levels in this class.

Circle Spells (Level 5).summary: Gain sleet storm and slow (3rd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 5).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: sleet storm and slow.

Circle Spells (Level 5).summary: Gain water breathing and water walk (3rd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 5).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: water breathing and water walk.

Circle Spells (Level 5).summary: Gain protection from energy and create food and water (3rd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 5).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: protection from energy and create food and water.

Circle Spells (Level 5).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: call lightning and plant growth.

Circle Spells (Level 5).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: lightning bolt and meld into stone.

Circle Spells (Level 5).summary: Gain stinking cloud and vampiric touch (3rd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 5).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: stinking cloud and vampiric touch.

Circle Spells (Level 5).summary: Gain gaseous form and stinking cloud (3rd-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 5).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: gaseous form and stinking cloud.

Wild Shape CR 2.summary: Maximum CR for Wild Shape increases to 2.
Wild Shape CR 2.description: At 5th level, the maximum Challenge Rating of beasts you can transform into with Wild Shape (Circle of the Moon feature) increases to 2.

Land's Stride.summary: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs no extra movement.
Land's Stride.description: Starting at 6th level, moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

Primal Strike.summary: Your attacks in beast form count as magical.
Primal Strike.description: Starting at 6th level, your attacks in beast form count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

Circle Spells (Level 7).summary: Gain conjure minor elementals and ice storm (4th-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 7).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: conjure minor elementals and ice storm.

Wild Shape Improvements.summary: Can transform into flying beasts with Wild Shape.
Wild Shape Improvements.description: At 8th level, you can transform into beasts with a flying speed when using Wild Shape, but you can’t have a flying speed greater than 30 feet.

Circle Spells (Level 9).summary: Gain commune with nature and cone of cold (5th-level spells).
Circle Spells (Level 9).description: You gain the following Circle Spells, which are always prepared: commune with nature and cone of cold.

Wild Shape CR 3.summary: Maximum CR for Wild Shape increases to 3.
Wild Shape CR 3.description: At 9th level, the maximum Challenge Rating of beasts you can transform into with Wild Shape (Circle of the Moon feature) increases to 3.

Nature's Ward.summary: Immune to poison/disease and resistant to elemental damage from plants.
Nature's Ward.description: At 10th level, you can't be poisoned or diseased. You also have resistance to the elemental damage (acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder) dealt by the plants and fungi of your chosen land type.

Nature's Ward.description: At 10th level, you can't be poisoned or diseased. You also have resistance to the elemental damage (acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder) dealt by the plants and fungi of your chosen land type.

Elemental Wild Shape.summary: Spend two Wild Shape uses to transform into an elemental.
Elemental Wild Shape.description: At 10th level, you can expend two uses of Wild Shape at the same time to magically transform into an Air Elemental, Earth Elemental, Fire Elemental, or Water Elemental.

Wild Shape CR 4.summary: Maximum CR for Wild Shape increases to 4.
Wild Shape CR 4.description: At 11th level, the maximum Challenge Rating of beasts you can transform into with Wild Shape (Circle of the Moon feature) increases to 4.

Pact Magic.summary: Cast warlock spells using Charisma. Spell slots restore on short/long rest.
Pact Magic.description: You can cast warlock spells using Charisma. You have a limited number of spell slots that recharge on a short or long rest. The spell slots are all of the same level, which is determined by your Warlock level (see the Warlock table).

The Fiend.description: You gain powers from a fiendish patron.

Dark One's Blessing.summary: Gain temporary HP on defeating a hostile creature.
Dark One's Blessing.description: When you reduce a hostile creature to 0 HP, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier + your warlock level.

The Archfey.description: You gain powers from an archfey patron.

Fey Presence.summary: Charm or frighten creatures within 10 feet.
Fey Presence.description: You can use your action to cause each creature of your choice within 10 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed or frightened by you until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature once per short or long rest.

The Great Old One.description: You gain powers from a mysterious entity.

Awakened Mind.description: You can telepathically communicate with any creature you can see within 30 feet. You don’t need to share a language with the creature, but it must be able to understand a language.

The Celestial.description: You gain powers from a celestial being.

Healing Light.summary: Use a bonus action to heal a creature.
Healing Light.description: You have a pool of d6s equal to 1 + your warlock level. As a bonus action, you can heal a creature you can see within 60 feet, spending dice from the pool up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one die). The creature regains hit points equal to the total rolled. The pool refreshes on a long rest.

The Hexblade.description: You gain powers from a sentient weapon.

Hexblade's Curse.summary: Curse a target for combat bonuses.
Hexblade's Curse.description: As a bonus action, you can curse a target you can see within 30 feet for 1 minute. You gain a bonus to damage rolls against the cursed target, your attack rolls against it score a critical hit on a 19 or 20, and if the target drops to 0 HP, you gain temporary HP equal to your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus.

The Genie - Dao.description: You gain powers from a dao genie patron.

Genie's Vessel.summary: Summon a vessel to channel your powers.
Genie's Vessel.description: You can summon a magical vessel that serves as a conduit for your genie patron's power. The vessel can take various forms, such as a lamp, ring, or bottle. While you have the vessel, you can use it to cast certain spells and gain other benefits granted by your patron.

The Genie - Djinni.description: You gain powers from a djinni genie patron.

Genie's Vessel.summary: Summon a vessel to channel your powers.
Genie's Vessel.description: You can summon a magical vessel that serves as a conduit for your genie patron's power. The vessel can take various forms, such as a lamp, ring, or bottle. While you have the vessel, you can use it to cast certain spells and gain other benefits granted by your patron.

The Genie - Efreeti.description: You gain powers from an efreeti genie patron.

Genie's Vessel.summary: Summon a vessel to channel your powers.
Genie's Vessel.description: You can summon a magical vessel that serves as a conduit for your genie patron's power. The vessel can take various forms, such as a lamp, ring, or bottle. While you have the vessel, you can use it to cast certain spells and gain other benefits granted by your patron.

The Genie - Marid.description: You gain powers from a marid genie patron.

Genie's Vessel.summary: Summon a vessel to channel your powers.
Genie's Vessel.description: You can summon a magical vessel that serves as a conduit for your genie patron's power. The vessel can take various forms, such as a lamp, ring, or bottle. While you have the vessel, you can use it to cast certain spells and gain other benefits granted by your patron.

Eldritch Invocations.summary: Choose two eldritch invocations to enhance your abilities.
Eldritch Invocations.description: At 2nd level, you gain two eldritch invocations of your choice. You can choose from a list of invocations that provide various benefits, such as improving your spellcasting or granting new abilities. You gain access to more invocations as you level up.
choices: Agonizing Blast(requires: eldritch blast cantrip):#When you cast Eldritch Blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.
Armor of Shadows:#You can cast mage armor on yourself at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.

Aspect of the Moon(requires: Pact of the Tome):#You no longer need to sleep and can't be forced to sleep by any means. To gain the benefits of a long rest, you can spend all 8 hours doing light activity, such as reading your Book of Shadows and keeping watch.
Beast Speech:#You can cast speak with animals at will, without expending a spell slot.
Beguiling Influence:#You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills.
Book of Ancient Secrets(requires: Pact of the Tome):#You can now inscribe magical rituals in your Book of Shadows.
Devil's Sight:#You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.
Eldritch Mind:#You have advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain concentration on a spell.
Eldritch Sight:#You can cast detect magic at will, without expending a spell slot.
Eldritch Spear(requires: eldritch blast cantrip):#When you cast eldritch blast, its range is increased to 300 feet.
Eyes of the Rune Keeper:#You can read all writing.
Fiendish Vigor:#You can cast false life on yourself at will as a 1st-level spell, without expending a spell slot or material components.
Gaze of Two Minds:#You can use your action to touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn.
Gift of the Ever-Living Ones (requires: Pact of the Chain feature):#Whenever you regain hit points while your familiar is within 100 feet of you, maximize the number of hit points restored.

Improved Pact Weapon(requires: Pact of the Blade feature):#Your pact weapon gains a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls, provided it isn't a magical weapon that already has a bonus to those rolls.
Lance of Lethargy(requires: eldritch blast cantrip):#When you hit a creature with eldritch blast, you can reduce that creature's speed by 10 feet until the end of your next turn.
Mask of Many Faces:#You can cast disguise self at will, without expending a spell slot.
Misty Visions:#You can cast silent image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.
Repelling Blast(requires: eldritch blast cantrip):#When you hit a creature with eldritch blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.
Thief of Five Fates:#You can cast bane once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

Voice of the Chain Master(requires: Pact of the Chain feature):#You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as they are on the same plane of existence as you.

Pact Boon.summary: Choose a Pact Boon: Pact of the Chain, Pact of the Blade, or Pact of the Tome.
Pact Boon.description: At 3rd level, your pact with your patron grants you a gift. Choose one of the following options.
choices: Pact of the Chain:#You learn the Find Familiar spell and can cast it as a ritual. The familiar can take any form, including special forms like Imp, Pseudodragon, Quasit, or Sprite.
Pact of the Blade:#You can use your action to magically create a pact weapon in your empty hand. You are proficient with it and use your Charisma modifier for attack and damage rolls.

Pact of the Tome:#Your patron gives you a Book of Shadows, a leather-bound tome. When you gain this feature, choose three cantrips from any class's spell list. While the book is on your person, you can cast those cantrips at will.

Eldritch Invocations Upgrade.description: At 5th level, you gain a third Eldritch Invocation. Some higher-level Invocations become available.

Thirsting Blade (requires: 5th level, Pact of the Blade):#You can attack with your pact weapon twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
Thief of Five Fates:#You can cast bane once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

Voice of the Chain Master(requires: Pact of the Chain):#You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as they are on the same plane of existence as you.

Dark One's Own Luck.summary: Add 1d10 to a saving throw or ability check once per short/long rest.
Dark One's Own Luck.description: Starting at 6th level, you can call on your patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add 1d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the initial roll but before any of the roll's effects occur. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

Misty Escape.summary: Turn invisible and teleport when you take damage.
Misty Escape.description: Starting at 6th level, when you take damage, you can use your reaction to turn invisible and teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You remain invisible until the start of your next turn or until you attack or cast a spell. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

Entropic Ward.summary: Impose disadvantage on an attack against you.
Entropic Ward.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on that roll. If the attack misses, your next attack roll against that creature has advantage. You can use this feature once per short or long rest.

Radiant Soul.summary: Add Charisma modifier to fire or radiant damage of any spell.
Radiant Soul.description: Starting at 6th level, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of any spell you cast that deals fire or radiant damage.

Accursed Specter.summary: Create a Specter from a slain humanoid.
Accursed Specter.description: Starting at 6th level, when you slay a humanoid, you can cause its spirit to rise as a Specter under your control. The Specter remains until you finish a long rest. You can have only one Specter at a time.

Fiendish Resilience.summary: Choose one damage type to gain resistance to until long rest.
Fiendish Resilience.description: Starting at 10th level, you can choose one damage type when you finish a short or long rest. You gain resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature. Damage from magical or silvered weapons ignores this resistance.

Beguiling Defenses.summary: Immune to being charmed, and can turn the charm back on the sender.
Beguiling Defenses.description: Beginning at 10th level, you are immune to being charmed, and when another creature attempts to charm you, you can use your reaction to attempt to turn the charm back on that creature.

Thought Shield.summary: Resistance to psychic damage; immune to mental probing.
Thought Shield.description: Starting at 10th level, your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it. You also have resistance to psychic damage.

Celestial Resilience.summary: Gain temp HP after a short/long rest.
Celestial Resilience.description: Starting at 10th level, you gain temporary hit points equal to your warlock level + your Charisma modifier whenever you finish a short or long rest.

Armor of Hexes.summary: Roll a d6 to negate damage from an attack against your cursed target.
Armor of Hexes.description: Starting at 10th level, whenever the creature affected by your Hexblade’s Curse hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to roll a d6. On a 4 or higher, the attack instead misses you, regardless of its roll.

Mystic Arcanum (6th Level).summary: Choose one 6th-level Warlock spell; cast once per long rest.
Mystic Arcanum (6th Level).description: At 11th level, your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called a Mystic Arcanum. Choose one 6th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

tools: Any one type of artisan's tools or any one musical instrument

Martial Arts.summary: Use Dex for unarmed/monk weapons, use bonus action for an extra unarmed strike.
Martial Arts.description: You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes and monk weapons. When you take the Attack action with an unarmed strike or a monk weapon on your turn, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action. Your unarmed strike damage starts at 1d4 and increases as you gain Monk levels (see Ki-fueled Attack table).

Unarmored Defense.summary: AC = 10 + Dex mod + Wis mod when unarmored/unshielded.
Unarmored Defense.description: While not wearing armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

Ki.summary: Gain a pool of Ki points (2 points, refreshes on short/long rest).
Ki.description: Your access to ki is represented by a number of ki points. Your Monk level determines the number of points you have. You start with 2 ki points. You can spend these points to fuel various Ki features (Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, Step of the Wind). You regain spent ki points when you finish a short or long rest.
choices: FLURRY OF BLOWS: #Immediately after you take the Attack action on yourturn, you can spend I ki point to make two unarmedstrikes as a bonus action.
PATIENT DEFENSE: #you can spend I ki point to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on your turn.
STEP OF THE WIND: #You can spend I ki point to take the Disengage or Dashaction as a bonus action on your turn, and your jumpdistance is doubled for the turn.

Unarmored Movement.description: Starting at 2nd level, your speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing armor or wielding a shield. This bonus increases as you gain Monk levels.

Deflect Missiles.summary: Use reaction to reduce ranged attack damage, or catch/throw it back.
Deflect Missiles.description: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your reaction to deflect or catch the missile when you are hit by a ranged weapon attack. When you do so, the damage you take from the attack is reduced by 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Monk level. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can spend 1 ki point to make a ranged attack with the weapon (range 20/60) as part of the same reaction.

Way of the Open Hand.description: A path of peace and mastery over Ki, focusing on combat maneuvers.

Open Hand Technique.summary: Apply extra effects on Flurry of Blows.
Open Hand Technique.description: Whenever you hit a creature with one of the attacks granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target: it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone; it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you; or it can't take reactions until the end of your next turn.

Way of Shadow.description: Monks who weave the Ki of shadow for stealth and illusion.

Shadow Arts.summary: Spend 2 Ki points to cast certain spells.
Shadow Arts.description: You can spend 2 ki points to cast one of the following spells without providing material components: darkness, darkvision, pass without trace, or silence.

Way of the Four Elements.description: Monks who harness the elemental Ki of air, earth, fire, and water.

Disciple of the Elements.description: You learn one elemental disciplines of your choice. A discipline requires you to spend ki points to use it. You can learn additional disciplines as you gain Monk levels.
choices: Ride the Wind(requires: 11th level):#You can spend 4 ki points to cast fly on yourself.
Flames of the Phoenix(requires: 11th level):#You can spend 4 ki points to cast fireball.

Water Whip:#You can spend 2 ki points as an action to create a whip of water that shoves and pulls a creature to unbalance it. A creature that you can see within 30 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes 3d10 bludgeoning damage, plus an extra 1d10 bludgeoning damage for each additional ki point you spend, and you can either knock it prone or pull it up to 25 feet closer to you. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and you don't pull it or knock it prone.
Eternal Mountain Defense(requires: 17th level):#You can spend 5 ki points to cast stoneskin on yourself.
Cauterizing Sweeping Strike:#You can spend 2 ki points to cast burning hands.
Gong of the Summit(requires: 6th level):#You can spend 3 ki points to cast shatter.
Rush of the Gale Spirits:#You can spend 2 ki points to cast gust of wind.

Shape the Flowing River:#As an action, you can spend 1 ki point to choose an area of ice or water no larger than 30 feet on a side within 120 feet of you. You can change water to ice within the area, and vice-versa, and you can reshape the ice in the area in any way you choose. You can raise or lower the ice's elevation, create or fill a trench, erect or flatten a wall, or form a pillar. The extent of these changes cannot exceed half the area's largest dimension. For example, if you affect a 30-foot square, you can create a pillar up to 15 feet high, raise or lower its elevation by 15 feet, dig a 15-foot deep trench, and so on. You cannot shape the ice to trap or injure a creature in the area.
Wave of Rolling Earth(requires: 17th level):#You can spend 6 ki points to cast wall of stone.
Mist Stance(requires: 11th level):#You can spend 4 ki points to cast gaseous form.

Fangs of the Fire Snake:#When you use the Attack action on your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to cause tendrils of flame to stretch out from your fists and feet. Your reach with your unarmed strikes increases by 10 feet for that action, as well as the rest of the turn. A hit with such an attack deals fire damage instead of bludgeoning damage, and if you spend 1 ki point when the attack hits, it also deals an extra 1d10 fire damage.

Fist of Unbroken Air:#You can create a blast of compressed air that strikes like a mighty fist. As an action, you can spend 2 ki points and choose a creature within 30 feet of you. The creature must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes 3d10 bludgeoning damage, plus an extra 1d10 bludgeoning damage for each additional ki point you spend, and you can push the creature up to 20 feet away from you and knock it prone. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and you don't push it or knock it prone.
Fist of Four Thunders:#You can spend 2 ki points to cast thunderwave.
River of Hungry Flame(requires: 17th level):#You can spend 5 ki points to cast wall of fire.
Clench of the North Wind(requires: 6th level):#You can spend 3 ki points to cast hold person.
Breath of Winter(requires: 17th level):#You can spend 6 ki points to cast cone of cold.

Elemental Attunement.summary: You can use your action to momentarily control the elemental forces nearby
Elemental Attunement.description: You can use your action to momentarily control the elemental forces nearby, causing one of the following effects of your choice: Create a harmless, instantaneous sensory effect related to air, earth, fire, or water, such as a shower of sparks, a puff of wind, a light mist, or a faint rumble of stone. Instantaneously light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a small campfire. Chill or warm up to 1 pound of nonliving material for up to 1 hour. Cause earth, fire, water, or mist that can fit within a 1-foot cube to shape itself into a crude form you designate for 1 minute.

Slow Fall.summary: Reduce falling damage by 5x your Monk level (reaction).
Slow Fall.description: Beginning at 4th level, you can use your reaction when you fall to reduce any falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.

Extra Attack.summary: Attack twice instead of once when you take the Attack action.
Extra Attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

Stunning Strike.summary: Spend 1 Ki point on a hit to force a Con save or be Stunned.
Stunning Strike.description: You can interfere with the flow of ki in an opponent's body. When you hit another creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 ki point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Wisdom modifier or be stunned until the end of your next turn).

Ki-Empowered Strikes.summary: Your unarmed strikes count as magical for overcoming resistance.
Ki-Empowered Strikes.description: Starting at 6th level, your unarmed strikes count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

Unarmored Movement Upgrade.description: At 6th level, your speed bonus from Unarmored Movement increases to 15 feet.

Wholeness of Body.summary: Heal 3x your Monk level as an action once per long rest.
Wholeness of Body.description: At 6th level, you can regain hit points equal to three times your Monk level as an action. You must finish a long rest before you can use this feature again.

Shadow Step.description: Upon reaching 6th level, when you are in dim light or darkness, you can use a bonus action to teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness. After you teleport, your first melee attack roll before the end of the turn has advantage.

Elemental Discipline Upgrade.description: You learn one additional Elemental Discipline of your choice.
choices: Ride the Wind(requires: 11th level):#You can spend 4 ki points to cast fly on yourself.
Flames of the Phoenix(requires: 11th level):#You can spend 4 ki points to cast fireball.
