Evasion.summary: Half damage on failed Dex saves, no damage on success.
Evasion.description: At 7th level, when you are subjected to an effect that requires you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

Stillness of Mind.summary: End the Charmed or Frightened condition (action).
Stillness of Mind.description: Starting at 7th level, you can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be charmed or frightened.

Unarmored Movement Improvement.summary: You can move across water and up vertical surfaces for 10 ft.
Unarmored Movement Improvement.description: Beginning at 9th level, you can move across the surfaces of water without sinking during your move, and you can move up vertical surfaces on your turn without needing to make an ability check. This applies only for the first 10 feet of movement.

Unarmored Movement Upgrade.description: At 9th level, your speed bonus from Unarmored Movement increases to 20 feet.

Purity of Body.description: At 10th level, your mastery of the ki flowing through you makes you immune to disease and the poisoned condition.

Monk Subclass Feature.description: You gain your next subclass feature at this level.

Tranquility.summary: Begin a long rest with Sanctuary effect on self.
Tranquility.description: Beginning at 11th level, you can enter a special meditation for 1 minute that immediately grants you the benefit of a short rest (but only one short rest can be taken this way per long rest). Additionally, when you finish a long rest, you gain the benefit of the Sanctuary spell that lasts until the start of your next long rest.

Cloak of Shadows.summary: Become invisible as an action in dim light/darkness.
Cloak of Shadows.description: By 11th level, you have learned to become one with the shadows. When you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you can use your action to become invisible.

Elemental Discipline Upgrade.description: You learn one additional Elemental Discipline of your choice.
choices: Ride the Wind(requires: 11th level):#You can spend 4 ki points to cast fly on yourself.
Flames of the Phoenix(requires: 11th level):#You can spend 4 ki points to cast fireball.

Sorcerous Origin.summary: Choose a sorcerous origin that grants you unique abilities.
Sorcerous Origin.description: At 1st level, you choose a sorcerous origin, which grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 14th, and 18th level.

Spellcasting.summary: Cast sorcerer spells using Charisma. Spells prepare on a long rest.
Spellcasting.description: You can cast sorcerer spells using Charisma. You know a number of spells as shown in the Sorcerer table. You have a limited number of spell slots that recharge on a long rest.

Draconic Bloodline.description: Your innate magic comes from draconic power.

Draconic Ancestry.summary: Choose a dragon type; gain resistance and bonus HP.
Draconic Ancestry.description: Choose a type of dragon (e.g., Red, Blue, Gold). You gain resistance to the damage type associated with that dragon. Additionally, whenever you are not wearing armor, your AC equals 13 + Dexterity modifier. You also gain 1 additional hit point for each level you gain in this class.
choices: Red Dragon
Blue Dragon
Green Dragon

Wild Magic.description: Your magic is chaotic and unpredictable.

Wild Magic Surge.summary: Roll on the Wild Magic Surge table after casting a spell.
Wild Magic Surge.description: Immediately after you cast a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, the DM can have you roll a d20. If you roll a 1, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to create a random magical effect.

Aberrant Mind.description: Your mind is touched by the Far Realm.

Telepathic Speech.summary: Communicate telepathically with any creature within 30 ft.
Telepathic Speech.description: You can communicate telepathically with any creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The creature doesn't need to share a language with you to understand your telepathic messages, but it must be able to understand at least one language.

Clockwork Soul.description: Your magic is derived from the orderly forces of Mechanus.

Clockwork Magic.summary: Gain additional spells at certain levels.
Clockwork Magic.description: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Clockwork Soul Spells table. These spells count as sorcerer spells for you, but they don't count against the number of sorcerer spells you know.

Font of Magic.summary: Gain Sorcery Points (2 points, refreshes on long rest).
Font of Magic.description: At 2nd level, you gain 2 sorcery points, which you can use to fuel your Metamagic options and other Sorcerer features. Your Sorcery Points total increases as you level up. You regain all expended sorcery points when you finish a long rest.

Metamagic.description: You gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You learn two Metamagic options of your choice.
choices: Careful Spell (1 SP): Protect allies from the blast of your spells.
Distant Spell (1 SP): Increase the range of a spell.
Empowered Spell (1 SP): Reroll a number of damage dice up to your Charisma modifier.
Extended Spell (1 SP): Double the duration of a spell up to 24 hours.
Heightened Spell (3 SP): Give a target disadvantage on a saving throw against your spell.
Quickened Spell (2 SP): Cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action as a bonus action.
Subtle Spell (1 SP): Cast a spell without verbal or somatic components.
Twinned Spell (1+ SP): Target a second creature with a spell that normally targets only one creature.

Spell Slot Progression.summary: You gain access to 3rd-level spell slots.

Sorcerous Origin Feature.summary: Gain your next Sorcerous Origin feature.
Sorcerous Origin Feature.description: You gain the feature determined by your Sorcerous Origin at this level.

Elemental Affinity.summary: Add Cha mod to one damage roll; spend 1 SP for resistance.
Elemental Affinity.description: When you cast a spell that deals damage of the type associated with your Draconic Ancestry, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell. Additionally, you can spend 1 sorcery point to gain resistance to that damage type for 1 hour.

Tides of Chaos.summary: Gain advantage on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw once per long rest.
Tides of Chaos.description: You can manipulate the forces of chance to gain advantage on one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Immediately after you do so, the DM can force a Wild Magic Surge on your next spell cast, and you regain the use of this feature.

Psionic Sorcery.summary: Spend 1 SP to cast a sorcerer spell as a psionic spell.
Psionic Sorcery.description: You can spend 1 sorcery point to cast a sorcerer spell you know without verbal or somatic components, treating it as a psionic spell.

Psionic Defenses.summary: Gain resistance to psychic damage and advantage on saves against being charmed or frightened.
Psionic Defenses.description: Starting at 6th level, you gain resistance to psychic damage. Additionally, you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

Bastion of Law.summary: Spend 1–5 sorcery points to create protective wards that reduce damage you take.
Bastion of Law.description: You can expend 1 to 5 sorcery points to create a magical ward around yourself or another creature you can see within 30 feet. The ward lasts until you finish a long rest or until you use this feature again. The ward is represented by a number of d8s equal to the sorcery points spent. When the warded creature takes damage, it can use its reaction to roll any number of those dice, reducing the damage taken by the total rolled. The ward loses dice equal to the number rolled.

Spell Slot Progression.summary: You gain access to 4th-level spell slots.
You gain access to 5th-level spell slots.

Metamagic Upgrade.description: You learn one additional Metamagic option of your choice.
choices: Careful Spell (1 SP): Protect allies from the blast of your spells.
Distant Spell (1 SP): Increase the range of a spell.
Empowered Spell (1 SP): Reroll a number of damage dice up to your Charisma modifier.
Extended Spell (1 SP): Double the duration of a spell up to 24 hours.
Heightened Spell (3 SP): Give a target disadvantage on a saving throw against your spell.
Quickened Spell (2 SP): Cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action as a bonus action.
Subtle Spell (1 SP): Cast a spell without verbal or somatic components.
Twinned Spell (1+ SP): Target a second creature with a spell that normally targets only one creature.

Spell Slot Progression.summary: You gain access to 6th-level spell slots.

tools: Three musical instruments of your choice

Bardic Inspiration.summary: Use a bonus action to give a creature a d6 die to add to a roll.
Bardic Inspiration.description: You can use a bonus action on your turn to choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet who can hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die (d6). Once within the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw. The number of dice you have is equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). You regain all uses on a long rest.

Spellcasting.description: You can cast bard spells using Charisma. You know a number of spells as shown in the Bard table. You have a limited number of spell slots that recharge on a long rest.

Jack of All Trades.summary: Add half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you aren't proficient in.
Jack of All Trades.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to any ability check you make that doesn’t already include your proficiency bonus.

Song of Rest.summary: Heal extra HP during a short rest (extra 1d6).
Song of Rest.description: Beginning at 2nd level, you can use soothing music or oration to help revitalize your wounded allies during a short rest. Any friendly creature that takes a short rest with you regains an extra 1d6 hit points. The die size increases as you gain Monk levels.

Bard College.description: At 3rd level, you delve into the advanced techniques of a bard college: the College of Lore or the College of Valor, both detailed below.

Bard Expertise.summary: Choose two skills to double your proficiency bonus on checks.
Bard Expertise.description: At 3rd level, choose two of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies. You choose two more skills at 10th level.

College of Lore.description: Bards who are masters of lore, knowledge, and cutting words.

Cutting Words.summary: Use a Bardic Inspiration die to subtract from a creature's attack roll, ability check, or damage roll.
Cutting Words.description: When a creature you can see within 60 feet makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one of your uses of Bardic Inspiration, rolling the die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll.

College of Valor.description: Bards who are skilled combatants and inspire courage in battle.

Combat Inspiration.summary: Bardic Inspiration can be added to damage rolls or AC.
Combat Inspiration.description: A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can choose to use it to increase its damage roll or its AC. The die is added to the damage roll made against a target or to the AC against a single attack.

Bardic Inspiration Upgrade (d8).summary: Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d8. Regain all uses on a short or long rest.
Bardic Inspiration Upgrade (d8).description: The Bardic Inspiration die changes from a d6 to a d8. Additionally, you regain all expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a short or long rest.

Song of Rest Upgrade (d8).summary: Song of Rest die becomes a d8.

Countercharm.summary: Use an action to grant advantage on saves vs. Frightened/Charmed.
Countercharm.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. As an action, you can start a performance that lasts until the end of your next turn. During that time, you and any friendly creatures within 30 feet of you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed. The effect ends early if you are incapacitated or silenced or if you voluntarily end it (no action required).

Additional Magical Secrets.summary: Learn two spells from any class's spell list.
Additional Magical Secrets.description: You learn two spells of your choice from any class's spell list. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as bard spells for you but don't count against the number of bard spells you know.

Extra Attack.summary: Attack twice instead of once when you take the Attack action.
Extra Attack.description: Beginning at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

Song of Rest Upgrade (d10).summary: Song of Rest die becomes a d10.

Bardic Inspiration Upgrade (d10).summary: Bardic Inspiration die becomes a d10.
Bardic Inspiration Upgrade (d10).description: The Bardic Inspiration die changes from a d8 to a d10.

Expertise Upgrade.summary: Choose two additional skills to gain Expertise (double proficiency).
Expertise Upgrade.description: At 10th level, choose two more of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.

Magical Secrets.summary: Learn two spells from any class's spell list.
Magical Secrets.description: You learn two spells of your choice from any class's spell list. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as bard spells for you but don't count against the number of bard spells you know.

Favored Enemy.summary: Choose a creature type to gain advantage on Survival/Intelligence checks to track/recall info.
Favored Enemy.description: You choose a type of favored enemy (e.g., beasts, fey, undead). You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track your favored enemy, as well as on Intelligence checks to recall information about them. When you gain this feature, you also learn one language of your choice that is spoken by the favored enemy, if they speak one.

Natural Explorer.summary: Choose a type of terrain; gain travel benefits in that area.
Natural Explorer.description: Choose one type of terrain (e.g., Arctic, Desert, Forest). While traveling for an hour or more in your favored terrain, you gain benefits like ignoring nonmagical difficult terrain, having advantage on initiative, and doubled travel pace.

fighting_style.summary: Choose a fighting style to specialize in.
fighting_style.description: At 2nd level, you adopt a style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options.

choices.Archery: You gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls made with a ranged weapon.
choices.Great Weapon Fighting: When you roll a 1 or a 2 on a damage die with a two-handed melee weapon, reroll the die.
choices.Two-Weapon Fighting: Add your ability modifier to the second attack when dual-wielding.
choices.Defense: While wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
choices.Dueling: When wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, gain a +2 bonus to damage.
choices.Protection: Use your reaction to impose disadvantage on an attack targeting an ally within 5ft if you're wielding a shield.

Spellcasting.description: You can cast ranger spells using Wisdom. You know a limited number of spells, as shown on the Ranger table.

Ranger Archetype.description: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you strive to emulate: Hunter or Beast Master.

Hunter.description: A focused archetype that excels at taking down specific threats.

Hunter's Prey.summary: Choose Colossus Slayer, Giant Killer, or Horde Breaker.
Hunter's Prey.description: You gain one of the following features of your choice:
choices:
**Colossus Slayer:** Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the creature takes an extra 1d8 damage if it's below its maximum hit points.



**Giant Killer:** When a Large or larger creature within 5 feet of you hits or misses you with an attack, you can use your reaction to attack that creature once.



**Horde Breaker:** Once per turn when you make a weapon attack, you can make an attack with the same weapon against a different creature within 5 feet of the original target.

Beast Master.description: A ranger who forms a powerful bond with an animal companion.

Ranger's Companion.description: You gain a beast companion that accompanies you on your adventures. The beast's CR can't be higher than 1/4. The companion acts on your initiative. You can command the beast to take the Attack, Dash, Disengage, or Help action as an action on your turn. If the beast is reduced to 0 HP, it doesn't die and can be healed after a long rest.

Extra Attack.summary: Attack twice instead of once when you take the Attack action.
Extra Attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

Coordinated Attack.summary: Your companion can attack when you take the Attack action.
Coordinated Attack.description: Starting at 5th level, when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks to allow your companion to make one attack of its own.

Favored Enemy Improvement.description: You choose one additional creature type as a favored enemy, along with an associated language.

Natural Explorer Improvement.description: You choose one additional type of favored terrain.

Ranger Archetype Feature.summary: Gain your next Ranger Archetype feature.
Ranger Archetype Feature.description: You gain the next feature determined by your Ranger Archetype at this level.

Defensive Tactics.summary: Choose Escape the Horde, Multiattack Defense, or Steel Will.
Defensive Tactics.description:
You gain one of the following features of your choice:

**Escape the Horde:** Opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.

**Multiattack Defense:** When a creature hits you with an attack, you gain a +4 bonus to AC against all subsequent attacks made by that creature for the rest of the turn.

**Steel Will:** You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

Exceptional Training.summary: Your companion gains improved abilities and saves.
Exceptional Training.description: Your companion's attacks count as magical for overcoming resistance. It also gains advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened, and its AC increases by 1.

Land's Stride.summary: Ignore nonmagical difficult terrain and resist movement effects from plants.
Land's Stride.description: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

Hide in Plain Sight.summary: Spend 1 minute to camouflage and gain +10 on Stealth checks.
Hide in Plain Sight.description: You can spend 1 minute to camouflage yourself. When you do so, you gain a +10 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks as long as you remain there without moving or taking actions. Once you move or take an action or reaction, you must camouflage yourself again.

Favored Enemy Improvement.description: You choose one additional creature type as a favored enemy, along with an associated language.

Natural Explorer Improvement.description: You choose one additional type of favored terrain.

Ranger Archetype Feature.summary: Gain your next Ranger Archetype feature.
Ranger Archetype Feature.description: You gain the next feature determined by your Ranger Archetype at this level.

Multiattack.description:
You gain one of the following features of your choice:

**Volley:** You can use your action to make a ranged attack against any number of creatures within 10 feet of a point you can see within the weapon's range. You must have ammunition for each target.

**Whirlwind Attack:** You can use your action to make a melee attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target.

Bestial Fury.summary: Your companion can make two attacks when commanded.
Bestial Fury.description: Starting at 11th level, when you command your companion to take the Attack action, the beast can make two attacks, or take the Multiattack action if it has that feature.
